findings.summary: The scan-only transactional bypass is implemented as a plain internal boolean on exported indexMemoryFile(), so its safety still depends on current call sites rather than a hardened API contract.
findings.recommended_fix: Replace fromScan with a scan-only wrapper, branded token, or equivalent internal-only contract so future callers cannot silently bypass the transactional recheck.

findings.summary: The packet still lacks the live acceptance rerun against 026/009-hook-daemon-parity, so the expected zero candidate_changed outcome after B2 is not yet operationally verified.
findings.recommended_fix: Restart the MCP runtime in an embedding-enabled environment, rerun memory_index_scan on 026/009-hook-daemon-parity, and record exact outcomes in the packet docs.

findings.summary: The PE downgrade logic now falls back from canonical_file_path to file_path, but there is no direct packet regression proving that behavior when the candidate row has a null canonical path.
findings.recommended_fix: Add a PE orchestration regression where the sibling candidate has canonical_file_path: null and only file_path populated.

findings.summary: The packet's fromScan regressions cover the real transactional bypass but do not directly exercise tenant/session-scoped saves even though scope shapes both candidate filtering and lineage logical keys.
findings.recommended_fix: Add a scoped fromScan regression that proves tenant/session-governed scan saves keep the intended reconsolidation and lineage behavior.
